items.headline: Major commercial development cleared at Rue Ferrari site
items.summary: A $35 million site work permit has been issued for Pld Athena Phase B, a significant commercial project in San José. This large-scale development represents substantial investment in the city's commercial real estate.

items.headline: Bank of America office renovation permitted on Winchester
items.summary: Bank of America received a $2.4 million tenant improvement permit for its office space at 560 S Winchester Boulevard. The project reflects ongoing corporate investment in San José's commercial districts.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This public art installation adds a named memorial piece to one of San José's major riverfront parks.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget approved for 2026-27
items.summary: Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget report and annual assessment. The BID funds improvements and services in the Eastridge commercial corridor.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. This element addresses how the city will handle public health, emergency preparedness, hazard mitigation, and safety infrastructure as part of its long-term community vision.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Milpitas City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent City Manager. This represents a significant leadership decision for the city's executive administration.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water management plans amid shortage risks
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed a draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. As California faces ongoing drought conditions, the city is preparing strategies to manage water supply and respond to potential shortages.

items.headline: Spring community grants and events applications under review
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications for the spring cycle on May 18. Residents can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood improvements.
